description.task: Create a SecurityFilterChain class that models Spring Security's filter chain concept. Filters are applied in order to incoming requests. Each filter can either pass the request to the next filter or reject it. Build a chain of authentication and authorization checks.
instructions: addFilter() appends a named Predicate<Request> to the filter list
process() iterates through all filters in registration order
If a filter's predicate returns false, immediately return FilterResult(false, filterName)
If all filters pass, return FilterResult(true, 'OK')
Filters are applied in the exact order they were added
description.hint: In process(), use a for loop over filters. Call entry.getValue().test(request). On false, return new FilterResult(false, entry.getKey()). After the loop, return FilterResult(true, 'OK').
description.expectedOutput:
Admin access: true
No token: false (AuthenticationFilter)
User restricted: false (AuthorizationFilter)

description.task: Implement a SecurityConfigBuilder class that models Spring Security's HttpSecurity configuration DSL. Build a configuration that specifies which URL patterns require authentication and which are publicly accessible.
instructions: permitAll() and requireAuthentication() add patterns to their respective lists and return 'this'
isAccessible() checks if the path matches any permitAll pattern — if so, return true regardless of authentication
If the path matches a requireAuthentication pattern, return true only if authenticated=true
Wildcard matching: a pattern ending in '/**' matches any path that starts with the prefix (e.g., '/public/**' matches '/public/home')
Exact patterns match only identical paths (e.g., '/login' matches only '/login')
description.hint: Create a helper pathMatches(String pattern, String path): if pattern ends with '/**', check path.startsWith(pattern.replace("/**", "")); else check pattern.equals(path).

description.task: Implement a CsrfTokenValidator class that simulates Spring Security's CSRF protection. The server generates a unique CSRF token per session, which clients must include in state-changing requests (POST, PUT, DELETE). Validate the token and report the result.
instructions: generateToken() creates a UUID token, stores it in sessionTokens keyed by sessionId, and returns it
validate() returns true immediately for any method in SAFE_METHODS
For non-safe methods, look up the session's stored token; return true only if submittedToken equals it
Return false if the sessionId is not found in sessionTokens
Return false if submittedToken is null (for non-safe methods)
description.hint: In validate(), first check SAFE_METHODS.contains(method.toUpperCase()). Then get the stored token, and return storedToken != null && storedToken.equals(submittedToken).
description.expectedOutput:
GET allowed: true
POST with valid token: true
POST with bad token: false
POST with no session: false
